title: Align docs and status narrative with V2.6

intent.problem: Repository-facing documentation still narrates AI Cockpit as if the story ends at V2.5, while the implemented scenario coverage signal and the current architecture are already V2.6-shaped. Several localized README links also point to the wrong installation page.
constraints: Keep the governance vocabulary consistent with .ai/glossary.md and existing cockpit terminology.
Do not change runtime behavior or generated status semantics beyond regenerating status after doc edits.
Do not expand scope into unrelated product or code changes.
intent.rationale: Readers should see one coherent version story across README, roadmap, architecture, and field documentation, with V2.6 scenario coverage and installation links pointing to the actual canonical path.

sources.reason: Defines the canonical V2.6 terminology for Repository Truth, Governance Compression, and Scenario Coverage.
Captures the intended version progression and the current milestone narrative.
Provides the canonical installation path that localized README files should link to.
Explains the generic scenario coverage policy mechanism that should be documented for Work Items.

problemStatement: The repository documentation is partially synced to V2.6 but still contains V2.5-era language, stale architecture flow, and broken installation links in the localized READMEs.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Version narrative reflects V2.6 across primary README entrypoints
scenarioCoverage.reason: Needs doc edits and a final review pass.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Architecture and field documentation describe Scenario Coverage and the current cockpit flow
scenarioCoverage.reason: Needs doc edits and a final review pass.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Localized installation links point to docs/getting-started/installation.md
scenarioCoverage.reason: Needs link updates in localized READMEs.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Scenario coverage policy source is documented for readers

riskAssessment.reason: This is a doc-only synchronization task with no expected runtime or code-path impact.

executionDecision.reason: The required sources are available and the scope is limited to documentation.

preReviewWarnings: Keep the README, roadmap, architecture, and contract-field terminology aligned rather than independently rewritten.
Regenerate generated status instead of hand-editing it if doc edits affect cockpit status.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Long-form doc alignment is prone to drift across multiple files.

acceptance: README.md, README.zh-CN.md, and README.ja.md describe V2 as completed/implemented and V2.5 as implemented while also surfacing V2.6 scenario coverage.
README.zh-CN.md and README.ja.md link to docs/getting-started/installation.md instead of the stale docs/installation.md path.
docs/roadmap.md no longer describes V2 as the current milestone; V2.5 is stabilizing/implemented and V2.6 is the current capability reference.
docs/architecture.md shows the current Intent -> Contract -> Implementation -> Verification -> Summary -> Cockpit -> Human Decision flow and includes Scenario Coverage in the core model.
docs/contract-fields.md places knownGaps in the Summary field section, fixes the commandHash wording, and documents scenario_coverage_policy.yaml as the generic policy source.
.ai/cockpit/README.md remains consistent with the updated V2.6 narrative and current status terminology.
guidelines: Prefer precise, user-facing terminology over historical shorthand when those terms conflict.
Keep cross-language README links canonical and identical where possible.
Treat policy files as generic mechanism descriptions, not as repositories of scenario content.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The task requires synchronized updates to repository documentation and cockpit-facing narrative files.

rollbackNote: Revert the doc diffs and regenerate cockpit status back to the pre-task state if necessary.
